lede: A look at June 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kylie Cantrall and Albert Costa, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 storming of the Kenyan Parliament
events.desc: Thousands of people storm Kenya's Parliament Buildings protesting the passing of the government's 2024/25 Finance Bill.

events.title: Khobar Towers bombing
events.desc: The Khobar Towers bombing in Saudi Arabia kills 19 U.S. servicemen.

events.title: Rainbow flag (LGBTQ)
events.desc: The rainbow flag representing gay pride is flown for the first time during the San Francisco Gay Freedom Day Parade.

events.title: Indira Gandhi
events.desc: Prime Minister Indira Gandhi declares a state of internal emergency in India.

events.title: Cryptography
events.desc: Cold War: Two cryptographers working for the United States National Security Agency left for vacation to Mexico, and from there defected to the Soviet Union.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: The Korean War begins with the invasion of South Korea by North Korea.

events.title: United States Congress
events.desc: The United States Congress passes the Displaced Persons Act to allow World War II refugees to immigrate to the United States above quota restrictions.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The French armistice with Nazi Germany comes into effect.

events.title: Gavriil Pribylov
events.desc: Gavriil Pribylov discovers St. George Island of the Pribilof Islands in the Bering Sea.

births.name: Kylie Cantrall
births.line: American actress, singer, songwriter, dancer, and social media personality

births.name: Willis Reed
births.line: American basketball player, coach, and manager (died 2023)

births.name: V. P. Singh
births.line: Indian lawyer and politician, 7th Prime Minister of India (died 2008)

deaths.text: Richard B. Sellars, American businessman and philanthropist
Michael Jackson, American singer-songwriter, producer, dancer, and actor
Thomas Eakins, American painter, photographer, and sculptor
Charles de Batz de Castelmore d'Artagnan, French captain

observances.text: Christian feast day: Our Lady of Medjugorje
Christian feast day: Philipp Melanchthon (Evangelical Lutheran Church in America)
Christian feast day: Presentation of the Augsburg Confession (Lutheran)
